name: io.github.KevinRabun/judges
title: Judges Panel
description: 37 judges that evaluate AI-generated code for security, cost, and quality with built-in AST.

tools.name: evaluate_code
tools.description: Submit code to the full 37-judge tribunal for security, cost, and quality analysis. Handles all code types including application code, infrastructure-as-code (Bicep, Terraform, ARM), and configuration files.

tools.name: evaluate_code_single_judge
tools.description: Run a single judge against code for targeted domain analysis. Handles all code types including infrastructure-as-code (Bicep, Terraform, ARM). Key domains: cybersecurity, data-sovereignty, iac-security, compliance, cost-effectiveness, authentication, cloud-readiness.

tools.name: evaluate_project
tools.description: Multi-file project analysis with cross-file architectural detection (duplication, dependency cycles, inconsistent error handling).

tools.name: evaluate_diff
tools.description: Evaluate only changed lines in a diff — filters findings to affected lines for PR reviews.

tools.name: analyze_dependencies
tools.description: Analyze PACKAGE MANAGER manifests only (package.json, requirements.txt, Cargo.toml, go.mod, pom.xml, .csproj) for supply-chain risks and version pinning issues. NOT for Bicep, Terraform, or infrastructure config — use evaluate_code or evaluate_code_single_judge for those.

tools.name: get_judges
tools.description: List all available judges with their IDs, names, and domains.

tools.name: evaluate_v2
tools.description: V2 context-aware tribunal evaluation with policy profiles, confidence scoring, and structured context ingestion.

tools.name: evaluate_app_builder_flow
tools.description: 3-step app-builder workflow: tribunal review, plain-language risk translation, and prioritized remediation tasks.

tools.name: evaluate_public_repo_report
tools.description: Clone a public repository URL, run the full judges panel, and generate a consolidated markdown report.

tools.name: fix_code
tools.description: Evaluate code and automatically apply all available auto-fix patches. Returns the fixed code along with a summary of applied and remaining findings.

tools.name: list_files
tools.description: List files and directories in the workspace. Useful for exploring project structure before evaluating code.

tools.name: read_file
tools.description: Read the contents of a file in the workspace. Returns the file text with optional line-range slicing.

tools.name: evaluate_with_progress
tools.description: Evaluate code with progressive judge-by-judge reporting. Returns intermediate counts as each judge completes, useful for large files.

tools.name: benchmark_gate
tools.description: Run the benchmark suite and check results against quality thresholds. Returns pass/fail with metric details. Use in CI to prevent quality regressions.

tools.name: compare_benchmarks
tools.description: Compare two benchmark runs and report metric deltas. Useful for verifying that code changes haven't degraded detection accuracy.

tools.name: evaluate_batch
tools.description: Evaluate multiple files in a single call with bounded concurrency. Returns per-file verdicts and aggregate statistics.

prompts.name: full-tribunal
prompts.description: Convene all 37 judges for a comprehensive LLM-powered deep review.

prompts.name: judge-{id}
prompts.description: Individual judge prompts (35 total) — use a specific judge's expert persona for targeted LLM-powered analysis.
